title: Stale-analyst-assessment overwrite guard: structural protection for operator-approved verdicts against re-integration of pre-existing analyst assessment files
authored_by: tinker (2026-05-11 run, in response to DIRECTIVE-20260510-003)
amends: decider Step 1h2 (Prediction Assessment Integration) — adds a timestamp-based stale-skip; test.js — adds a verdict_history-vs-current-verdict consistency check (NEVER_PUSH; operator-applied)
target_files: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md (Step 1h2 — adds stale-check before each assessment is integrated; agent-applied)
test.js (new assertion: every entry with verdict_history must have entry.our_verdict === verdict_history[last].to; NEVER_PUSH — operator-applied)
monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md (document the stale-skip semantics and the verdict_history-consistency invariant)

executive_summary.problem_one_line: On 2026-04-18 commit 209fda52, the decider integrated four PRED-assessment-*.json files dated 2026-04-11 that silently reverted four operator-applied verdict changes from 2026-04-14 (commit a05ebcad / ISS-951/952/953/954); the structural signal needed to catch this — `predictions.json[id].verdict_history[last].at` was newer than the assessment file's `assessed_at` — was already in the data and nobody read it.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Belt-and-suspenders Mech AB: (A) decider Step 1h2 reads `verdict_history[last].at` for each prediction and skips integration with attention-inbox notice if the recorded history is newer than the assessment's `assessed_at`; (B) test.js adds a pre-push invariant that `our_verdict === verdict_history[last].to` for every entry that has a verdict_history — catching the corruption at the very last moment if Mech A is ever bypassed.

tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q1_where_guard_lives: Mech AB (both layered). Mech A is the live save — it stops the bad write at the source and routes the operator a one-line attention-inbox notice. Mech B is the catastrophic safety net — if Mech A is bypassed (decider prompt edited away, new agent variant introduced, integration moved to a different code path) the pre-push gate still catches the inconsistency before it ships. The cost of Mech B is one node block in test.js — trivial. Layering matches PROP-014 / PROP-022 / PROP-026 architecture.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q2_trip_signal: Timestamp comparison only — `verdict_history[last].at` (predictions.json side) vs `assessed_at` (assessment-file side). If `verdict_history[last].at > assessed_at`, the assessment is stale; skip integration. No closed-issues.json lookup required — verdict_history is the authoritative trace and operator already writes to it whenever a verdict changes (verified: commit a05ebcad's data/predictions.json after-state has the verdict_history entry sitting right there). Closed-issues lookup would be redundant and would require N-day-lookback heuristics. Timestamp comparison is mechanical and self-documenting.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q3_on_trip_action: Skip-with-attention (NOT silent-skip; NOT escalate-to-pending-human). Silent-skip is what produced 209fda5 in the first place — the integration was effectively a no-op on the operator's behalf but no one was warned. Escalate-to-human floods the inbox during normal prediction churn. The right middle path is: skip the write, push a single line to monitor/decisions/attention-inbox.json with the prediction_id, the stale assessment file, both timestamps, and the action 'analyst should re-run if a fresh assessment is needed.' One attention-inbox line per skip; not an ISS.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q4_HNOTE_interaction: The timestamp-based check naturally handles HNOTE-driven verdict changes. When an HNOTE (e.g. HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001) fires, the analyst writes a fresh EXP-*.json (e.g. EXP-301, `written_at: 2026-05-10T08:14:02.050Z`). The decider integrates that EXP and the verdict_history entry it writes is dated 2026-05-10. On the NEXT decider run, the freshly-written verdict_history entry is older than any subsequent analyst output, so subsequent assessments don't trip the guard. The HNOTE channel and structural guard are genuinely belt-and-suspenders: HNOTE creates the fresh assessment, structural guard prevents stale ones from undoing it.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q5_archive_lookback_scope: Live `predictions.json` only — no archive read needed. The `verdict_history` array travels INSIDE the prediction entry; it is not in closed-issues.json or its archive. PROP-022 phase 4 archive split affects closed-issues only; predictions.json is unaffected by archive and stays a single live file. The check reads exactly one field per integrated assessment: `entry.verdict_history?.at(-1)?.at`. No archive plumbing required.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q6_integrity_gate_factoring: Fold ISS-1201 into PROP-028 as Mech B (the test.js consistency assertion). ISS-1201 asks for a pre-push gate on `our_verdict === verdict_history[last].to`; that's exactly the Mech B check Q1 recommends. Two separate test additions would be confusing — one assertion captures both Mech B and ISS-1201's request. The assertion runs for every entry with a verdict_history (currently 4 entries in predictions.json, 3 in wins.json — extend to both files).

executive_summary.scope_clarification: This PROP authorizes design only. Mech A is agent-applied (decider prompt edit, NOT NEVER_PUSH). Mech B is operator-applied (test.js IS NEVER_PUSH). Operator reviews and applies.

context_verification.investigation_method: Cloned repo fresh into /tmp/edit-clone-tinker; ran git show 209fda52, git show 209fda52^:data/predictions.json, git show a05ebcad, and pulled the four ISSs from closed-issues.json; read assessment files from monitor/analyst/expansions/.
context_verification.directive_date_note: DIRECTIVE-003 cites '209fda5 (2026-04-20)' — the actual commit is dated 2026-04-18T18:28:53. Operator's recollection was approximate; the failure mode is exactly as described. Noting the date correction for the record.
context_verification.directive_iss_attribution_note: DIRECTIVE-003 says the analyst assessments that produced 209fda5 had dates of 2026-04-11. Confirmed: all four PRED-assessment-*.json files have `assessed_at` between 2026-04-11T05:24Z and 2026-04-11T08:11Z (sync timestamps match too).

failure_mode_reconstruction.summary: Four operator-approved verdict changes were silently reverted in a single decider self-apply run, four days after the operator applied them. The structural signal needed to detect the staleness was already in the data — it just wasn't being read.

timeline_utc.action: Wrote monitor/analyst/expansions/PRED-assessment-W019.json with our_verdict='standard_physics', reasoning about NOAA NMP drift being standard geophysics.
Wrote PRED-assessment-PRED-TOROID-003.json with our_verdict='unfalsifiable' and PRED-assessment-W020.json with our_verdict='unfalsifiable'.
timeline_utc.note: These four assessment files all stamped 2026-04-11; sync to git happened 2026-04-11T14:42-15:09Z (workspace-sync sequence).

timeline_utc.action: Reviewed PRED-batch-2026-04-13. Recommended verdict reversals: W019 should be 'falsified' (poleward drift was DOMINATE, observed lateral 5.5x larger); W020 should be 'falsified' (≥3% threshold specified, criterion failed); PRED-NEW-002 should be 'standard_physics' (9.2 km/s within PREM P-wave 8-13 km/s range).
Created ISS-951/952/953 from curmudgeon review; tagged 'Verdict change requires human approval'. Status='open', severity='major'/'moderate', category='verdict'.
Created ISS-954 for PRED-TOROID-003 after finding two fatal derivation errors in the dome's Schumann splitting math; recommended unfalsifiable → standard_physics.
Operator approved all four verdict changes.

Pushed commit a05ebcad 'Apply 5 verdict change approvals (human decisions)' — wrote our_verdict, our_verdict_prev, AND verdict_history[] entries for all four predictions. Each verdict_history entry has at='2026-04-14T05:31:57.174Z' and reason='Human approval (ISS-95N): <reasoning>'.
timeline_utc.key_observation: The verdict_history field was correctly populated by the operator's commit. The structural signal needed to detect future stale-overwrites was sitting in the data from 2026-04-14 onward.

timeline_utc.action: Self-apply pass walked monitor/analyst/expansions/PRED-assessment-*.json (the same files written 7 days earlier on 2026-04-11), did NOT check predictions.json[id].verdict_history[last].at vs assessment.assessed_at, and integrated each assessment's our_verdict into predictions.json — silently reverting all four operator changes.
timeline_utc.diff_effect: W019: falsified → standard_physics (reverted); W020: falsified → unfalsifiable (reverted); PRED-NEW-002: standard_physics → unfalsifiable (reverted); PRED-TOROID-003: standard_physics → unfalsifiable (reverted).
timeline_utc.verdict_history_corruption: Because the integration didn't append a new verdict_history entry, the post-209fda5 state has `our_verdict !== verdict_history[last].to` for all four entries — the consistency invariant ISS-1201 calls for is violated and would be detected by a pre-push test.

structural_signal_that_was_ignored.delta: verdict_history entry is 3-4 days NEWER than the assessment file in every case
structural_signal_that_was_ignored.decision_rule: if verdict_history.at(-1).at > assessment.assessed_at → assessment is stale → skip integration with attention-inbox notice
structural_signal_that_was_ignored.post_check_consistency: after Mech A skip, our_verdict and verdict_history[last].to remain equal (operator's last write is preserved); Mech B's pre-push assertion holds

soft_mitigation_currently_in_place_since.mechanism: HNOTE dual-confirm protocol — operator-applied verdict changes file an explicit HNOTE that authorizes the change and prompts agents to write fresh EXP/assessment files in response.
soft_mitigation_currently_in_place_since.observed_effectiveness: No silent overwrites observed in the 3 weeks since 2026-04-20. Recent verdict changes (WIN-013/014 → Refuted by Data, WIN-055 → Self-Contradicted) all went through the HNOTE channel and have fresh EXP files dated 2026-05-09/10.
soft_mitigation_currently_in_place_since.structural_limitation: Depends on operator discipline (file the HNOTE) and agent integration discipline (read the HNOTE when authoring EXPs). Not a code-level invariant. If either side slips, the failure mode returns.

Q1_where_guard_lives.rationale: Mech A (decider integration check) prevents the bug at write time — the right place for a live save. Mech B (pre-push gate in test.js) catches the bug if Mech A is ever bypassed by a future prompt edit, new agent variant, or alternate code path. The cost of Mech B is ~10 lines in test.js; the cost of NOT having it is another 209fda5-style silent revert that ships to the public site.
Q1_where_guard_lives.alternative_considered: Mech A alone (cheaper, no operator-applied step). Rejected because the 209fda5 failure mode demonstrates that single-mechanism guards on critical data invariants get bypassed when integration code paths evolve. Belt-and-suspenders is the established pipeline pattern (PROP-014 state verification, PROP-022 archive integrity, PROP-026 dual-mode decider).
Q1_where_guard_lives.alternative_considered_2: Mech B alone (no decider-side check). Rejected because Mech B catches the bug AFTER the commit is staged — the decider has already written the bad data, the run report misrepresents what happened, and the pre-push fails with no graceful recovery path. Mech A catches it at write time and routes a clean attention-inbox notice; Mech B is the safety net.

Q2_trip_signal.answer: Timestamp comparison only: verdict_history[last].at vs assessed_at
Q2_trip_signal.rationale: The verdict_history field is the authoritative trace of every verdict change. Operator writes to it (verified in commit a05ebcad); decider should write to it on integration (current behavior: NOT writing — that's the consistency bug ISS-1201 flags). Comparing two timestamps is mechanical, self-documenting in the data, and trip-or-not-trip with no ambiguous heuristics.
alternatives_considered: Closed-issues.json lookup by prediction_id within last N days. Rejected: requires choosing N (operator's intuition was 'within last 30d'), creates a coupling between predictions.json and closed-issues.json maintenance, and is redundant — every closed verdict-change ISS already has a corresponding verdict_history entry on predictions.json since 2026-04-14. The verdict_history field is the cleaner single source.

Explicit HNOTE-confirmation lookup. Rejected: HNOTE channel is a process discipline, not a data invariant. If HNOTEs are missed or named differently, the check fails silently. The verdict_history field is set unconditionally by operator on every verdict change.
assessment-file-created-at as the time signal. Rejected: file mtime on FUSE is unreliable; `assessed_at` is the agent's own self-stamped time and is durable across syncs.
Q2_trip_signal.edge_case_no_verdict_history: If an entry has no verdict_history (legacy entries; new entries written by analyst before operator has ever touched them), the check is a no-op — there's nothing to override and the assessment is allowed to integrate. Predictable: a NEW prediction's first assessment integrates normally.

Q3_on_trip_action.rationale: Silent-skip is what produced 209fda5 — no warning, no audit trail, the operator only notices when they happen to re-read the page. Escalate-to-pending-human floods the queue during normal prediction churn (which spawns 3-5 assessments per analyst run). Attention-inbox is the middle ground: one line per skip, machine-readable, the operator sees it on their next decider summary read.

attention_inbox_record_shape._comment: Append one entry per skip; analyst marks resolved on re-run
attention_inbox_record_shape.action_recommended: If a fresh assessment is needed, re-run analyst on this prediction; otherwise leave as-is (current verdict was operator-approved at the recorded verdict_history time).

Q3_on_trip_action.alternative_considered: Escalate-to-pending-human via new ISS. Rejected for the routine-churn case but PROP-027's routing matrix would natively support 'ROUTE-TO-ANALYST' as the cleaner classification — if any specific skip warrants follow-up beyond the attention-inbox line, the operator can promote it manually. The default should be cheap.

Q4_HNOTE_interaction.answer: Natural compatibility — the timestamp check does not trip on HNOTE-driven assessments because they're fresh

verification_against_current_traffic.integration: Decider integrates 2026-05-10; will write verdict_history with at=2026-05-10T... — later than any prior assessment file
verification_against_current_traffic.guard_behavior: Mech A skip never trips because EXP-301 is newer than the historical verdict_history entry; correct integration proceeds.

verification_against_current_traffic.fresh_artifact: Operator writes verdict_history entry with at=2026-05-10T...
verification_against_current_traffic.guard_behavior: If a NEW analyst assessment of WIN-055 is written 2026-05-11 or later → integrates normally (assessment newer than verdict_history). If an OLD analyst assessment from before 2026-05-10 surfaces → Mech A skips and posts attention-inbox notice. Exactly the desired behavior.

Q4_HNOTE_interaction.no_circular_dependency: Mech A reads predictions.json (or wins.json — see below); Mech B reads predictions.json and wins.json. Neither writes data; both observe data. No risk of guard-self-referencing-guard.

Q5_archive_lookback_scope.rationale: verdict_history is INSIDE the prediction entry, not external. PROP-022 phase 4 archive split affects closed-issues.json only (separate file). The check reads exactly one field on the live prediction/win: entry.verdict_history.at(-1).at. Live file is the complete record.
Q5_archive_lookback_scope.N_days_recommendation: N/A — the check is not N-day-bounded. It compares two specific timestamps. If verdict_history has an entry from a year ago, that's still the most recent verdict change; if an assessment file is from before that change, it's stale regardless of absolute age.

Q6_integrity_gate_factoring.answer: Fold ISS-1201 into Mech B (one combined test.js assertion)
Q6_integrity_gate_factoring.test_js_assertion_one_line_summary: For every predictions.json/wins.json entry with a non-empty verdict_history, assert entry.our_verdict === entry.verdict_history.at(-1).to.
captures_two_things: Mech B safety net for Mech A bypass: if anyone integrates an assessment without writing the verdict_history entry, our_verdict diverges from verdict_history.at(-1).to and the assertion fails at pre-push
Q6_integrity_gate_factoring.extends_to_both_files: predictions.json (4 current entries with verdict_history) AND wins.json (3 current entries with verdict_history). Same invariant, same assertion shape — duplicate the for-loop or factor a helper.
Q6_integrity_gate_factoring.wins_format_note: wins.json verdict_history entries use 'date' field (YYYY-MM-DD) where predictions.json uses 'at' field (ISO datetime). Mech A timestamp comparison should accept either — read whichever is present, compare lexically (YYYY-MM-DD < YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ works since the 11th-char position is either end-of-string or a T which is > digit). Mech B consistency check doesn't care about timestamps at all — it only checks `to` equals `verdict`/`our_verdict`.

mech_a_decider_intake_step_1h2_edit.ownership: git-owned, agent-applied (decider can self-apply on next run by following the standard prompt-edit-then-commit pattern via clean clone)
mech_a_decider_intake_step_1h2_edit.current_location: Step 1h2: Prediction Assessment Integration (line 197-242 in current file)
mech_a_decider_intake_step_1h2_edit.proposed_edit: Insert between current step 1 (read assessment) and step 2 (update predictions.json) a new sub-step '1a: Stale-Assessment Check' with this logic:
mech_a_decider_intake_step_1h2_edit.prose_addition_for_prompt: Add to Step 1h2 a `Stale-Skip Protocol` subsection explaining: (a) the check compares verdict_history[last].at with assessment.assessed_at; (b) on stale, skip + attention-inbox; (c) on integrate, ALWAYS append a verdict_history entry so the invariant `our_verdict === verdict_history.at(-1).to` always holds after this step.
mech_a_decider_intake_step_1h2_edit.verification_pattern: After implementation, run `grep -n 'Stale-Skip\|STALE-SKIP\|verdict_history' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md` and confirm the section exists. Also run test.js (Mech B) which becomes the structural check.
mech_a_decider_intake_step_1h2_edit.ROLLBACK_path: If Mech A causes false positives (assessments correctly skipped because verdict_history advanced under HNOTE flow but operator intended re-assessment), the skip is observable via attention-inbox lines. Operator can re-stamp the assessment file's assessed_at (or have analyst re-write it) to bypass — no code change needed.

mech_b_test_js_consistency_assertion.ownership: git-owned, NEVER_PUSH (operator-applied per CLAUDE.md File Ownership table)
mech_b_test_js_consistency_assertion.insertion_point: After existing prediction-entry validation loop (around line 580 in current file, between the prediction loop closing brace and the HTML placeholder check)
mech_b_test_js_consistency_assertion.current_test_count_baseline: Run `node test.js 2>&1 | grep -oP '\d+ passed' | head -1` BEFORE applying; record the count. After application, count should increase by exactly the number of entries with non-empty verdict_history (currently 4 predictions + 3 wins = 7 new assertions).
mech_b_test_js_consistency_assertion.verification_pattern: (1) After applying, `node test.js` should pass with +7 (current data is consistent). (2) Inject a deliberate inconsistency in a TEST entry (do NOT commit) and confirm the assertion fires with the entry-id-tagged message. (3) Revert the deliberate inconsistency.
mech_b_test_js_consistency_assertion.NEVER_PUSH_implication: Per CLAUDE.md File Ownership, test.js is in NEVER_PUSH (workspace-sync deny-list); only the operator can apply this edit by editing test.js in the clean clone and pushing. The exact diff is specified above so the application is mechanical.

data_schemas_md_documentation.addition: Add a 'verdict_history consistency invariant' subsection under the predictions.json and wins.json schema sections documenting: (a) verdict_history entries are append-only; (b) every change to our_verdict/verdict MUST also append a new verdict_history entry; (c) the invariant our_verdict === verdict_history.at(-1).to is enforced by test.js (PROP-028 Mech B); (d) the assessed_at-vs-verdict_history.at(-1).at stale-skip is enforced by decider Step 1h2 (PROP-028 Mech A).
data_schemas_md_documentation.purpose: Single-page reference for any future agent or human reviewer to understand the invariant without spelunking through prompts.

ISS-1199.title_summary: decider-side fix proposal: cross-check closed-issues.json before integration
ISS-1199.retired_by: Mech A (timestamp comparison is a structurally stronger version of closed-issues-lookup — it reads the verdict_history field that lives ON the prediction entry, not an external file)
ISS-1199.close_rationale: PROP-028 Mech A implements a cleaner check than the original ISS-1199 design proposed. Closed-issues.json lookup was a fallback when verdict_history wasn't being written reliably; verdict_history IS being written reliably (verified from a05ebcad), so the cleaner check supersedes the fallback.

ISS-1200.title_summary: documented failure mode and root-cause attribution
ISS-1200.retired_by: PROP-028 context section (failure_mode_reconstruction) is the authoritative root-cause writeup with timeline, file paths, hash references
ISS-1200.close_rationale: PROP-028 incorporates the failure-mode documentation as living context for the structural fix.

ISS-1201.title_summary: integrity-side: pre-push gate that our_verdict matches verdict_history[last].to
ISS-1201.retired_by: Mech B (the test.js assertion is exactly the pre-push gate ISS-1201 requested)
ISS-1201.close_rationale: PROP-028 Mech B IS the implementation of ISS-1201's design. Closes on operator-applied test.js edit.

iss_retirement_plan.ordering: All three close in the same decider run that lands PROP-028 implementation — likely two-phase: Mech A self-applies first (closes ISS-1199, ISS-1200), then operator applies Mech B test.js diff and closes ISS-1201 with reference to commit hash.

considered_and_rejected.design: Closed-issues.json lookup by prediction_id within last N days
considered_and_rejected.rationale: Already-implemented verdict_history field is cleaner. Adding the closed-issues lookup would duplicate signal and require N-day-window tuning. If verdict_history is ever NOT written on a verdict change, the right fix is to make sure it gets written (Mech A's step 2 enforces this), not to add a parallel check.
considered_and_rejected.what_we_would_do_instead_if_verdict_hist: Then closed-issues lookup would be necessary. Current verification shows verdict_history is reliably written by both operator (a05ebcad) and decider (when implementing Mech A's step 2).

considered_and_rejected.rationale: Q3 analysis: routine prediction churn produces 3-5 assessments per analyst run. Escalating each stale-skip would flood the pending-human queue (which PROP-026 backlog-clearance is actively trying to reduce). Attention-inbox notice is sufficient — the operator sees one line per skip and decides if anything needs follow-up.

considered_and_rejected.design: Silent skip with no audit trail
considered_and_rejected.rationale: Silent-skip recreates the 209fda5 failure mode in mirror — the operator wouldn't see that their work was being silently no-op'd by stale assessments. Attention-inbox is the structurally correct outcome — every skip is visible and replayable.

considered_and_rejected.design: Wontfix all three ISSs; rely on HNOTE soft-mitigation indefinitely
considered_and_rejected.rationale: Considered per task_4 of directive. Rejected because: (a) the soft mitigation depends on operator discipline (file HNOTE on every change) and agent discipline (read HNOTE on every assessment authoring) — both have failure modes that won't be visible until the next 209fda5; (b) the cost of Mech AB is ~30 lines of code total + one operator-applied test.js diff — trivial relative to the cost of one silent verdict revert shipping to the public page; (c) the structural guard makes the soft mitigation belt-and-suspenders rather than load-bearing — both layers protect, neither is sole-of-failure.

considered_and_rejected.design: Mech A only (no Mech B test.js gate)
considered_and_rejected.rationale: Considered. Rejected because Mech A lives in a prompt that can be edited; future agents or prompt refactors could remove it without anyone noticing. Mech B is a code-level invariant that survives prompt churn. Cost of Mech B is one block in test.js; benefit is permanent.

Considered. Rejected because Mech B catches the bug AFTER the decider has already written the bad data into the working tree — the run report misrepresents what happened, the pre-push fails with an opaque message, and the decider has to retry. Mech A catches at write time with a clean attention-inbox notice and the run proceeds normally.

interaction_with_existing_props.PROP-009r2_queue_id_strict_matching: No interaction — PROP-028 operates on predictions.json/wins.json integration; PROP-009r2 governs queue_id matching for curmudgeon review files.
interaction_with_existing_props.PROP-014_state_verification: Aligned — PROP-014 established verify-on-read for state coupling; PROP-028 extends the same principle to predictions.json verdict consistency. Mech A is a verify-before-write; Mech B is a verify-on-state. Both fit the PROP-014 architecture.
interaction_with_existing_props.PROP-016_NEVER_PUSH_guard: Aligned — Mech B's test.js edit is correctly identified as NEVER_PUSH (operator-applied). PROP-016 stranded-patch convention applies if Mech A self-apply includes test.js edits by accident; PROP-028 specifies the boundary clearly.
interaction_with_existing_props.PROP-022_state_file_archive: No interaction — predictions.json is unaffected by closed-issues archive split; Mech A/B both read live predictions.json only.
interaction_with_existing_props.PROP-026_burndown_mode: Aligned — Mech A's attention-inbox notice is the right cheap routing for stale-skips; PROP-026's M1 matrix could optionally promote a STALE-ASSESS attention-inbox line to an ISS if it persists unresolved for >7d, but that's a follow-up if observed.
interaction_with_existing_props.PROP-027_5_action_routing_matrix: Aligned — STALE-ASSESS attention-inbox lines route to analyst when unresolved (analyst re-runs the assessment with the operator's recent state). PROP-027's ROUTE-TO-ANALYST is the natural action class if escalation is ever needed.
interaction_with_existing_props.DIRECTIVE-20260502-001_agent_state_coupl: Aligned — PROP-028 is a direct child of the state-coupling thesis: assessments and verdicts must be coupled by timestamp, not just by file existence.

operator_review_questions.q: Mech A self-apply: are you comfortable with the decider self-applying a prompt-file edit to decider-intake.md (it's git-owned, normal workflow)?
operator_review_questions.tinker_recommendation: Yes — this is the standard pattern. Decider can do this in its next run after PROP-028 lands, with a normal commit message.

operator_review_questions.q: Mech B test.js: do you want the exact diff above committed as a single operator-applied commit, or would you prefer to author it yourself with the design above as reference?
operator_review_questions.tinker_recommendation: Apply mechanically — the diff is specified precisely and follows existing test.js style. Should pass on current data without any edits.

operator_review_questions.q: Attention-inbox routing: PROP-026/PROP-027 are actively reducing the pending-human queue. Is the attention-inbox the right cheap signal, or do you want stale-skips to surface as a normal ISS (would route through PROP-027's matrix as ROUTE-TO-ANALYST)?
operator_review_questions.tinker_recommendation: Attention-inbox for the routine case. If a STALE-ASSESS line sits unresolved for >7d (analyst hasn't re-run), promote to ISS at that point. Avoids early ISS creation during routine churn.

operator_review_questions.q: Backfill ISS-1201 violations: post-209fda5, the four predictions had our_verdict != verdict_history.at(-1).to until commit 026ce27a restored them. If the operator later restored them, does the post-restore predictions.json now satisfy the Mech B invariant?
operator_review_questions.tinker_recommendation: Verified: current data/predictions.json on main HEAD has all four entries with consistent our_verdict and verdict_history.at(-1).to (operator's 026ce27a restoration appended a new verdict_history entry per prediction). Mech B will pass on current data with no backfill needed.

verification_pattern_for_post_landing_ti.mech_b_landed_check: node test.js 2>&1 | grep -q 'PROP-028 invariant' && echo 'Mech B landed'
verification_pattern_for_post_landing_ti.next_tinker_followup: On next Mode 2 / Mode 3 run, verify the three patterns above all pass; if any fail, the implementation is incomplete and PROP-028 stays in pending-operator-review until resolved.
